Dr. Walter "Larry" Lok

personal site

BA, Mathematics, University of Southern Mississippi, 1976.

M.A. Mathematics, Columbia University, 1979.

Ph.D. Mathematics, Columbia University, 1984.

Thesis title "Deformations of Locally Homogenous Spaces and Kleinian Groups".

Post Ph.D. faculty experience at Cornell University and the University of Southern Mississippi.

9 years post Ph.D. industrial experience at Intergraph, Inc., and Veribest, Inc, writing design and digital simulation software.

Current research projects include modeling biological processes using Markov processes, simulation of reactions involving many simultaneous reactions and reactants, application of shape theory to protein interactions, and various statistical aspects of the above work.
